question_text: A metal bar with a mass of 500 g occupies a volume of 50 cm³. What is the density of this metal, and is it likely to float in water (density of water = 1 g/cm³)?
correct_answer: Density = 10 g/cm³. The metal will not float because its density (10 g/cm³) is greater than the density of water (1 g/cm³).
explanation: Using density = mass ÷ volume: 500 g ÷ 50 cm³ = 10 g/cm³. Objects only float if their density is less than the fluid they are in, so this dense metal will sink in water.
